name: TransAlmeria gravel circular to Velez-Blanco
description:
The fourth stage of this Collection is totally optional, since it is a circular route that begins and ends in Vélez-Blanco itself. Furthermore, it is almost entirely a route along dirt tracks, with steep slopes and which in some places are not in very good condition. It also does not pass through towns, so you must carry with you everything you need for the day. If you are one of those who like strong emotions also in gravel and your tires have enough grip and width, you can leave your luggage at the accommodation and enjoy the mountain landscape of the Sierra de María - Los Vélez natural park.

You leave Vélez-Blanco towards the east, with the imposing Muela Grande on the horizon. This rocky plateau of more than 1,460 meters above sea level is located in the Sierra del Gigante. The Route borders the mountain range through the shade of Las Muelas, to continue northwards, towards the Sierra Larga. At kilometer 41 you enter a loop that surrounds the Gabar hill, where there are also caves with rock paintings. This circular is done along wide forest tracks in good condition, but if you want to avoid it, simply continue south, back to Vélez-Blanco.

Back in the town, you can visit its magnificent castle, which combines Gothic and Renaissance architecture and houses a museum of contemporary art. I also recommend visiting the convent of San Luis, and above all, taking a walk through its streets and admiring some of its unique houses, such as the Corredera house or the theater/hermitage of San Agustín.
region_intro: Gravel biking in Sierra María-Los Velez offers diverse terrain across 22,670 hectares, ranging from arid plains to rocky summits and densely wooded pine forests. The landscape features significant elevation changes, from 800m to over 2,000m, with limestone outcrops and deep ravines. This region provides a mix of unpaved tracks and quiet roads suitable for varied gravel cycling experiences. Best gravel bike trails in Sierra María-Los Velez The most popular gravel bike trail is TransAlmeria gravel circular to Velez-Blanco, a 42.5 miles (68.4 km) trail that takes 7 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's varied terrain, including both arid and forested sections. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Castillo de Vélez-Blanco – Muela Grande loop from Vélez-Blanco, a difficult 22.8 miles (36.7 km) path. This route offers views of Vélez-Blanco Castle and traverses mountainous paths. Local gravel bikers also love the Vélez-Blanco Castle – View of Sierra de María loop from Vélez-Blanco, a 14.7 miles (23.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with views of the Sierra de María, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Gravel biking in Sierra María-Los Velez is defined by arid plains, pine forests, and limestone terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature demanding climbs. The routes in Sierra María-Los Velez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 10 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Sierra María-Los Velez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: There are over 5 gravel bike trails available in Sierra María-Los Vélez, offering diverse experiences across the natural park's varied terrain.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for gravel biking in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: Most gravel bike trails in Sierra María-Los Vélez are rated as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;. They often feature demanding climbs and significant elevation changes, making them suitable for experienced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309869231/transalmeria-gravel-circular-to-velez-blanco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;TransAlmeria gravel circular to Velez-Blanco&lt;/a&gt; covers 68.4 km with over 1300m of elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: The terrain in Sierra María-Los Vélez is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter everything from arid, moon-like plains to densely wooded pine forests and rocky, limestone outcrops. Many routes combine unpaved tracks with quiet tarmac roads, offering a mix of surfaces and challenging ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31497511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Vélez-Blanco – Muela Grande loop from Vélez-Blanco&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 36.7 km circular path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez-Blanco Castle – View of Sierra de María loop from Vélez-Blanco&lt;/a&gt; offers a shorter 23.7 km option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: The park experiences extreme climate variations. While summer offers warm, dry rides, winters can bring snow-capped trails, especially at higher altitudes. Spring and autumn generally provide more moderate temperatures, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes without extreme heat or cold.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along gravel bike trails in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks. You can often spot the magnificent 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7263595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vélez Blanco Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The park is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6134128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Inscriptions&lt;/a&gt; (Cueva de los Letreros), a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its prehistoric cave paintings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7263595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Umbría de la Virgen Botanical Garden&lt;/a&gt; is another notable attraction showcasing local flora.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the gravel routes?
A: Yes, the varied topography of Sierra María-Los Vélez provides numerous scenic viewpoints. You'll find breathtaking vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2074254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sierra de María&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal del Morral Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the dramatic landscape, from arid plains to forested slopes.

Q: Is Sierra María-Los Vélez suitable for family-friendly gravel biking?
A: Given that most routes are rated as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt; with significant elevation gains, many trails might be challenging for younger children or families seeking very easy rides. However, the dedicated cyclist track along the A-317 between Vélez Blanco and María, which is off-limits to vehicles, could offer a more accessible option for families, though it leads into the hills.

Q: Are there options for parking near the gravel bike trails?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, the region's traditional villages, such as Vélez-Blanco and María, typically offer parking facilities. It's advisable to check local information for designated parking spots when planning your ride.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: The gravel biking experience in Sierra María-Los Vélez is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the opportunity to explore remote areas and traditional villages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the gravel bike trails in Sierra María-Los Vélez?
A: As Sierra María-Los Vélez is a Natural Park, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the presence of diverse fauna. Always check specific park regulations regarding pets before your visit.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to rest near the gravel bike trails?
A: The routes often pass through or near traditional villages like Vélez-Blanco, María, and Chirivel. These villages typically offer local amenities, including cafes and restaurants, where you can refuel and rest during your ride.
tips:
From Cantarerías Street in Vélez-Blanco, you can enjoy spectacular views of the Vélez-Blanco Castle. This street offers a unique perspective of the castle, allowing you to appreciate its imposing structure and strategic location on top of the hill.

Built in the 16th century, Vélez-Blanco Castle is one of the most outstanding monuments in the region. Its Renaissance architecture and fascinating history make it a must-see for visitors.

Los Caños de Caravaca is a public fountain located in Vélez-Blanco. It was commissioned by the Marquis of Los Vélez in the 16th century. It has four water pipes and an ornamentation that dates back to 1944, in which orange and green tiles were placed, and two pictorial scenes: one inspired by a scene from Don Quixote and another from "La Vendimia" by Francisco de Goya, in addition of the shield of the population that presides over the fountain.

The waters that flow down this limestone ravine have pierced and denuded the stone for millennia, leaving an enchanting landscape. Despite the little current that flows down the channel most of the year, the surroundings are full of life. The landscape is very curious.
Built between 1506 and 1515 by order of Pedro Fajardo y Chacón, first Marquis of Vélez, Vélez-Blanco Castle is a fascinating destination for lovers of history and architecture.

waypoints.name: View of Vélez-Blanco and its castle
